---
title: "Lecture 4 Quiz: HTML Grouping and Semantic Elements"
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

::: {.callout-note}
Review before quiz:
[Slides](/presentations/lecture-04.qmd) | [Week 1 Content](/content/week-01.qmd) | [Lab 1](/labs/lab-01.qmd) | [Assignment 1](/assignment/01-assignment.qmd)
:::

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
using QuizQuestions
```

## Lecture 4 Self-Check

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. <span>", "B. <div>", "C. <em>", "D. <strong>"]
question = "Question 1: Which element is a generic block-level container?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. <span>", "B. <section>", "C. <article>", "D. <main>"]
question = "Question 2: Which element is an inline generic container?"
answer = 1
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. <div>, <span>, <b>", "B. <font>, <center>, <big>", "C. <u>, <i>, <tt>", "D. <header>, <article>, <footer>"]
question = "Question 3: Which set contains semantic HTML elements?"
answer = 4
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Hides page structure", "B. Removes navigation landmarks", "C. Gives meaningful content structure", "D. Disables CSS rules"]
question = "Question 4: Semantic markup usually helps search engines and assistive tools because it:"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. <aside>", "B. <article>", "C. <span>", "D. <br>"]
question = "Question 5: If content is self-contained and reusable (like a blog post), which semantic tag is often best?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```
